<objective>
Close HARD-05: a worker dispatched through `--driver subprocess` completes or fails — it never blocks on an stdin nobody will ever write.

Purpose: today `spawn("bash", ["-lc", cmd], { cwd })` takes Node's default `stdio: "pipe"`, so the child's fd 0 is a pipe whose write end tickmarkr holds open forever and never writes. `codex exec` — given a prompt argument AND a piped stdin — reads fd 0 and appends it as a `<stdin>` block (its own `--help` says so). That read never returns. The worker burns the full `taskTimeoutMinutes` (default 30) per attempt and then stalls into consult. That is EXP-06.

Output: a committed live-probe diagnosis artifact, a one-line seam fix, and two red-capable oracles that fail on today's HEAD.

**D-04 disposition (stated, not left dangling): the won't-implement hatch is CLOSED BY EVIDENCE.** Research ran a zero-token live probe against real codex 0.144.1 (dead base_url + non-repo cwd → never reaches inference): under today's `pipe` it prints `Reading additional input from stdin...` and hangs (killed at 15s); under `stdio: ["ignore","pipe","pipe"]` it EOFs instantly and exits 1 in 74ms with a real surfaced error. The root cause IS on fd 0 and IS addressable at the driver seam. **This phase ships the fix.**
</objective>

<interfaces>
<!-- Contracts the executor needs. Do not go exploring — these are the whole surface. -->

src/drivers/subprocess.ts (the seam, today):
  export const MAX_BUF = 2 * 1024 * 1024;
  export class SubprocessDriver implements ExecutorDriver {
    id = "subprocess";
    interactive = false;               // no pane, no operator
    slot(cwd: string, name: string): Promise<Slot>;
    run(slot: Slot, cmd: string): Promise<void>;           // line 35 = the spawn site
    waitOutput(slot: Slot, pattern: string, timeoutMs: number, opts?: { regex?: boolean }): Promise<boolean>;
                                        // returns FALSE on timeout by design — never throws, never hangs
    waitAgentStatus(slot: Slot, status: string, timeoutMs: number): Promise<boolean>;
    read(slot: Slot, lines: number): Promise<string>;
    close(slot: Slot): Promise<void>;   // SIGKILLs a still-running child
  }

src/run/daemon.ts:290-293 (how a headless worker is actually composed and awaited — the D-05 trace):
  await driver.run(slot, `${inv.command}; ${exitMarkerCmd}`);
  finished = await driver.waitOutput(slot, `TICKMARKR_EXIT_${nonce}:\\d`, cfg.taskTimeoutMinutes * 60_000, { regex: true });
  output = await driver.read(slot, 500);
  const result = adapter.parse(output, nonce);   // no TICKMARKR_RESULT trailer → ok:false → gates fail loud
  // NOTE: `finished` keys on the EXIT MARKER, not on the trailer. This is why the fix cannot
  // trade a hang for a silent wait: post-fix the child always exits, so the marker always prints.

vitest.config.ts:
  testTimeout: 20000
  coverage thresholds "src/drivers/**": { lines: 82, branches: 80 }   // must not regress (D-09)
</interfaces>
</context>

  </read_first>

  <action>
  Reproduce the hang against TODAY'S UNFIXED spawn semantics and commit the transcript. This task lands BEFORE the fix (D-01).

  Run the zero-token live probe verbatim from 35-RESEARCH.md § "Live diagnosis probe" — it spawns real `codex exec` twice from `/tmp` with `model_provider=dead` / `base_url=http://127.0.0.1:9/v1`, so codex bails on its trusted-directory check before any inference. Zero tokens. Variant A = today's default stdio (`{}`); variant B = `stdio: ["ignore","pipe","pipe"]`.

  **No fallback.** 35-RESEARCH.md § Environment Availability confirms `codex` 0.144.1 is installed. If the binary is missing, FAIL THE TASK LOUDLY — do not substitute a stand-in and do not paste the transcript out of 35-RESEARCH.md. The `Reading additional input from stdin` line is emitted only by the real codex binary; producing it any other way is fabricated evidence, which is exactly what the honest-close invariant forbids. The gate below asserts on that line precisely because only a real run can produce it.

  Write `35-DIAGNOSIS.md` with, at minimum:
  - **Root cause**, one paragraph: Node's default `stdio: "pipe"` leaves fd 0 as a pipe whose write end tickmarkr holds open and never writes; `bash -lc` passes fd 0 straight through to the grandchild; `codex exec` with a prompt arg ALSO reads stdin (`codex exec --help`: "If stdin is piped and a prompt is also provided, stdin is appended as a `<stdin>` block") → an fd-0 read that never returns.
  - **The probe transcript**, pasted literally — it must contain the line `Reading additional input from stdin...` under variant A (the block) and the fast non-zero exit under variant B.
  - **Honest bound**: the block is not literally infinite at the daemon level — `waitOutput` is capped by `taskTimeoutMinutes` (default 30), so a stdin-blocked worker burns the FULL task timeout per attempt and then stalls into consult. Never write "hangs forever".
  - **Rejected alternatives** (measured, from research): `stdio: "inherit"` ALSO hangs → reject; `p.stdin.end()` works but is order-dependent → reject; `openSync("/dev/null")` works but adds fd bookkeeping → reject. Chosen: `stdio: ["ignore","pipe","pipe"]`.
  - **No TTY-branch risk**: fd 0 is NOTTY both before and after, so no agent CLI can flip an `isatty(0)` branch because of this fix.
  - **D-04 disposition**: a heading containing the literal string `CLOSED BY EVIDENCE` stating the won't-implement hatch does not apply and the fix ships.

  Then populate `tests/fixtures/scope-seam/legit-selfwrite/35-VALIDATION.md` — today it is an unfilled template (`nyquist_compliant: false`, a placeholder `{pytest 7.x / …}` framework cell, `REQ-{XX}` rows). Fill it from 35-RESEARCH.md § "Validation Architecture", which already carries every value: framework `Vitest 3.2.7`, config `vitest.config.ts`, quick command `npm test -- tests/drivers/subprocess.test.ts`, full command `npm test`, and the Requirements → Test map (HARD-05 × the two new oracles + the herdr D-10 regression + the D-09 coverage floor). Set `nyquist_compliant: true` and `wave_0_complete: true` — the Wave 0 gap (the two oracles) is closed by Task 2 in this same plan. Leave NO `{...}` or `REQ-{XX}` placeholder behind.
  </action>

<task type="auto" tdd="true">
  <name>Task 2: RED → GREEN — pin the hang, then close it at the spawn site (HARD-05)</name>

  <files>tests/drivers/subprocess.test.ts, src/drivers/subprocess.ts, tests/fixtures/scope-seam/legit-selfwrite/35-DIAGNOSIS.md</files>

  <read_first>
  - src/drivers/subprocess.ts (the file being modified — line 35 is the spawn site)
  - tests/drivers/subprocess.test.ts (the file being modified — 4 existing tests must keep passing)
  - tests/fixtures/scope-seam/legit-selfwrite/35-RESEARCH.md § "Code Examples" (the two oracles + the red-proving drill, verbatim)
  - tests/fixtures/scope-seam/legit-selfwrite/35-DIAGNOSIS.md (Task 1's output — the root cause this task closes)
  </read_first>

  <behavior>
  Two NEW tests in `tests/drivers/subprocess.test.ts`, both using a codex-SHAPED stand-in (never the real codex CLI — D-08, `npm test` stays zero-token):

  - Test A — "HARD-05: a worker that reads stdin reaches its trailer (no stdin hang)":
    run `cat >/dev/null; printf 'TICKMARKR_''EXIT_x:0\n'` — the `TICKMARKR_''EXIT` string-split keeps the source
    token from matching a grep for its own marker. Apply the SAME split in Test B (W3: both, or neither). Assert
    `await d.waitOutput(slot, "TICKMARKR_EXIT_x:\\d", 3000, { regex: true })` is `true`, then `await d.close(slot)`.
    The stand-in MUST read fd 0 to EOF BEFORE printing its marker — that ordering is what makes it red-capable.
    Pre-fix: the write end is held open forever, `cat` never EOFs, the marker never prints → `false` at 3000ms.
    Post-fix: fd 0 is `/dev/null`, `cat` EOFs instantly → marker in ~10ms.

  - Test B — "HARD-05/D-05: a stdin-reading worker that emits no trailer exits loudly, it does not wait":
    run `cat >/dev/null; printf '\nTICKMARKR_''EXIT_x:%s\n' $?` (same split as Test A), assert the exit marker appears within 3000ms, then `await d.read(slot, 100)` and
    assert the output does NOT match `/TICKMARKR_RESULT/`. This pins the D-05 trace: post-fix the child EXITS, so
    the daemon's headless wait (which keys on the exit marker, not the trailer — daemon.ts:291) completes,
    `parseWorkerResult` finds no trailer, fails closed → `ok:false` → gates fail LOUD. The fix converts an
    infinite block into a surfaced failure, never into a silent wait.

  Timeout bound is 3000ms — well under vitest's `testTimeout: 20000`. RED is therefore a clean ~3s
  `expected false to be true`, NOT a 20s suite hang. `close()` SIGKILLs the still-blocked child on the red path.
  </behavior>

  <action>
  Work RED → GREEN. The RED half is an executable drill, not narration.

  **STEP 1 (RED, mandatory, before touching `src/`).** Add the two tests above to `tests/drivers/subprocess.test.ts`. Do NOT modify `src/drivers/subprocess.ts` yet. Run — capturing to a file with color DISABLED, because vitest colorizes the summary count when stdout is a TTY and the ANSI escapes it injects between `Tests` and `2 failed` would make the gate's own grep unmatchable:
      NO_COLOR=1 npm test -- tests/drivers/subprocess.test.ts > /tmp/hard05-red.txt 2>&1; cat /tmp/hard05-red.txt
  EXPECT: both new tests FAIL in ~3s each with `expected false to be true`; the 4 pre-existing tests still pass. Copy the RED proof (below) out of `/tmp/hard05-red.txt`, never out of terminal scrollback.

  **Then COMMIT that redness as evidence (D-02 + D-12).** Gates run only AFTER the fix has landed — they see the final green diff and exit statuses, and `src/gates/acceptance.ts` shows the judge only a diff + the criteria. So "the tests were red on HEAD" is a WORKER CLAIM unless it is written to a file a gate can grep. Append the LITERAL vitest failure output (unedited: the `expected false to be true` lines, both failing test names, and the `Tests  2 failed` summary line) to `tests/fixtures/scope-seam/legit-selfwrite/35-DIAGNOSIS.md` under a new heading `## RED proof (D-02)`, together with the `git rev-parse HEAD` of the unfixed commit the drill ran against. Do not paraphrase it. This is the one artifact that distinguishes a real red-first close from the v1.7 TEL-01 scar.
  **If either new test is GREEN here, STOP.** The oracle is vacuous (the v1.7 TEL-01 scar class) — the stand-in is not actually reading fd 0 before its marker. Fix the oracle, re-run, and only proceed once it reddens.

  **STEP 2 (GREEN).** Apply the one-line seam fix at `src/drivers/subprocess.ts:35`. Change

      spawn("bash", ["-lc", cmd], { cwd: slot.cwd })

  to pass the explicit stdio tuple `["ignore", "pipe", "pipe"]` — i.e. `{ cwd: slot.cwd, stdio: ["ignore", "pipe", "pipe"] }`. `"ignore"` IS `/dev/null` in Node, so a child's read of fd 0 EOFs immediately instead of blocking. Keep the `stdout`/`stderr` `"pipe"` entries — the buffer capture on lines 37-38 is unchanged.

  Add a comment above the spawn naming the root cause and the ceiling, in the house style: this driver declares `interactive = false` (no pane, no operator), so an open stdin pipe is a promise tickmarkr can never keep; `codex exec` appends a piped stdin as a `<stdin>` block per its own `--help` and blocks on a read that never EOFs; fixing it at the single spawn site covers EVERY adapter at once (D-06 — per-adapter `< /dev/null` patching is the named anti-pattern, it leaves sibling adapters broken).

  Do NOT add a cast or a `!`. Research verified `tsc --noEmit` exits 0 with this exact change: the `stdio` tuple literal narrows the overload to `ChildProcessByStdio<null, Readable, Readable>`, whose `stdout`/`stderr` remain non-null, and `SlotState.proc?: ChildProcess` still accepts it.

  Do NOT touch `src/drivers/herdr.ts` (D-10) — visible-pane workers keep their interactive stdin. Do NOT add any dependency (D-07). Do NOT introduce a watchdog/reaper (explicitly deferred).

  **STEP 3 (verify).** Run, in order:
      npm test -- tests/drivers/subprocess.test.ts   # both new tests green in ~10ms; 4 existing still green
      npm test                                        # full suite, incl. tests/drivers/herdr.test.ts (D-10)
      npx tsc -p tsconfig.json --noEmit               # exit 0
      npm run test:coverage                           # src/drivers/** floor 82 lines / 80 branches holds (D-09)
  </action>

  <verify>
    <automated>grep -q "## RED proof (D-02)" tests/fixtures/scope-seam/legit-selfwrite/35-DIAGNOSIS.md && grep -q "expected false to be true" tests/fixtures/scope-seam/legit-selfwrite/35-DIAGNOSIS.md && grep -qE "Tests +2 failed" tests/fixtures/scope-seam/legit-selfwrite/35-DIAGNOSIS.md && grep -q 'stdio: \["ignore", "pipe", "pipe"\]' src/drivers/subprocess.ts && [ "$(git hash-object src/drivers/herdr.ts)" = "b46e9bb8d42b3a38c3ebe783840f5673ab3b7ecb" ] && [ "$(git hash-object package.json)" = "64b761ed4794ff38c120f8e46d80a26036416462" ] && npm test -- tests/drivers/subprocess.test.ts && npm test && npx tsc -p tsconfig.json --noEmit && npm run test:coverage</automated>
  </verify>

A gate can grep this; a worker cannot satisfy it by narrating. (Red on HEAD: the heading and the failure text do not exist.)
  - `src/drivers/subprocess.ts` contains the literal `stdio: ["ignore", "pipe", "pipe"]` at the `spawn("bash", ["-lc", cmd], ...)` site. (Red on HEAD: today the file has no `stdio` option at all.)
  - `npm test -- tests/drivers/subprocess.test.ts` exits 0 (6 tests: 4 pre-existing + 2 new).
  - `npm test` exits 0 — the full suite, including `tests/drivers/herdr.test.ts`, which is untouched (D-10).
  - `npx tsc -p tsconfig.json --noEmit` exits 0 with no cast and no `!` added.
  - `npm run test:coverage` exits 0 — `src/drivers/**` holds its 82-lines / 80-branches floor (D-09).
  - **`src/drivers/herdr.ts` is NOT modified (D-10) and `package.json` is NOT modified (D-07) — pinned by content hash, not asserted:** `git hash-object src/drivers/herdr.ts` == `b46e9bb8d42b3a38c3ebe783840f5673ab3b7ecb` and `git hash-object package.json` == `64b761ed4794ff38c120f8e46d80a26036416462`. (The scope gate alone does NOT cover this: `src/gates/scope.ts` passes out-of-scope edits whenever the worker declares any deviation, so an immutability claim needs its own oracle. A content hash is also commit-count-independent — a `HEAD~N` range check is not, since a tickmarkr worker makes an arbitrary number of commits.)
  </acceptance_criteria>

  <done>Both new tests were observed RED against unfixed HEAD, then GREEN after the one-line stdio fix. Full suite, tsc, and coverage floors all green. `src/drivers/herdr.ts` untouched.</done>

</tasks>

<threat_model>
## Trust Boundaries

| Boundary | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| tickmarkr → spawned worker child (fd 0/1/2) | tickmarkr hands a shell command to an agent CLI it does not control and inherits whatever fd wiring Node defaults to. |

## STRIDE Threat Register

| Threat ID | Category | Component | Disposition | Mitigation Plan |
|-----------|----------|-----------|-------------|-----------------|
| T-35-01 | Denial of Service | `SubprocessDriver.run` spawn site | mitigate | fd 0 → `/dev/null` via `stdio: ["ignore","pipe","pipe"]`. A worker can no longer take a run hostage by blocking on an stdin tickmarkr will never write. This is HARD-05 itself. |
| T-35-02 | Denial of Service | The fix's own failure mode (D-05) | mitigate | Pinned by Test B: post-fix the child EXITS, the daemon's exit-marker wait completes, `parseWorkerResult` fails closed → loud gate failure. No hang→silent-wait swap. |
| T-35-03 | Information Disclosure | Inherited operator stdin (`stdio: "inherit"`) | mitigate | Explicitly rejected (and measured to hang anyway). N concurrent headless workers must never contend for the operator's terminal. |
| T-35-04 | Tampering | Shell injection via command strings | accept | Unchanged by this phase — command strings still flow through the existing `shq()` quoting. No new input path is opened; the fix strictly REMOVES a child's ability to read an inherited fd 0. |
| T-35-SC | Tampering | npm installs | accept | Zero packages installed (D-07). No registry surface. |
</threat_model>
